Asset must already be configured in CollateralManager."},"liquidate(address,address,address,uint256)":{"details":"Flow:           1. Verify HF < 1.0           2. Cap debtAmount at 50% of debt (close factor)           3. Calculate collateral to seize (debtUsd / collateralPrice * (1 + bonus))           4. Reduce borrower's debt + collateral           5. Transfer debtAsset from liquidator \u2192 pool           6. Transfer collateralAsset from pool \u2192 liquidator","params":{"borrower":"Address of the position to liquidate.","collateralAsset":"The asset the liquidator will receive.","debtAmount":"Amount of debt to repay (capped at CLOSE_FACTOR).","debtAsset":"The asset the borrower owes."}},"renounceRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"repay(address,uint256)":{"returns":{"repaid":" Actual amount repaid."}},"revokeRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"supportsInterface(bytes4)":{"details":"See {IERC165-supportsInterface}."},"withdraw(address,uint256)":{"returns":{"withdrawn":" Actual amount transferred to the caller."}}},"title":"LendingPool","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"borrow(address,uint256)":{"notice":"Borrow `amount` of `asset` against deposited collateral.         Health factor must remain >= 1.0 after the borrow."},"deposit(address,uint256)":{"notice":"Deposit `amount` of `asset` into the pool.         Receive lTokens representing the deposit + accrued interest."},"initAsset(address)":{"notice":"Initialise a new asset in the pool."},"liquidate(address,address,address,uint256)":{"notice":"Liquidate an undercollateralised position."},"repay(address,uint256)":{"notice":"Repay up to `amount` of debt on `asset`.         Pass type(uint256).max to repay all outstanding debt."},"withdraw(address,uint256)":{"notice":"Withdraw up to `amount` of `asset`.         Pass type(uint256).max to withdraw everything."}},"notice":"Core protocol contract \u2014 handles deposits, borrows, repayments,         withdrawals, and liquidations. \u2500\u2500\u2500 Interest Model \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500   Every asset has two indices that start at RAY (1.0) and only increase:     liquidityIndex  \u2014 multiplied into scaled deposits \u2192 depositor's current value     borrowIndex     \u2014 multiplied into scaled debts    \u2192 borrower's current debt   On every state-changing call, _accrueInterest() runs first:     newBorrowIndex   = oldBorrowIndex * (1 + borrowRate * dt)     newLiquidityIndex = oldLiquidityIndex * (1 + supplyRate * dt) \u2500\u2500\u2500 Scaled Balances \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500   scaledDeposit[user][asset] = deposit / liquidityIndexAtDepositTime   currentDeposit             = scaledDeposit * currentLiquidityIndex   scaledBorrow[user][asset]  = borrow  / borrowIndexAtBorrowTime   currentDebt                = scaledBorrow  * currentBorrowIndex \u2500\u2500\u2500 Security \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500   \u2022 ReentrancyGuard on all state-mutating externals   \u2022 Health factor enforced before every borrow and withdrawal   \u2022 Oracle staleness checked on every price read (inside PriceOracle)   \u2022 Close factor: liquidator can repay at most 50% of debt per call   \u2022 Liquidation only when HF < getRoleAdmin(bytes32).details: Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}.

grantRole(bytes32,address).details: Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event.

initAsset(address).details: Deploys a fresh LendingToken receipt token for this asset.         Asset must already be configured in CollateralManager.

liquidate(address,address,address,uint25.details: Flow:           1. Verify HF < 1.0           2. Cap debtAmount at 50% of debt (close factor)           3. Calculate collateral to seize (debtUsd / collateralPrice * (1 + bonus))           4. Reduce borrower's debt + collateral           5. Transfer debtAsset from liquidator → pool           6. Transfer collateralAsset from pool → liquidator

params.borrower: Address of the position to liquidate.
params.collateralAsset: The asset the liquidator will receive.
params.debtAmount: Amount of debt to repay (capped at CLOSE_FACTOR).

renounceRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

revokeRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

returns.withdrawn: Actual amount transferred to the caller.

borrow(address,uint256).notice: Borrow `amount` of `asset` against deposited collateral.         Health factor must remain >= 1.0 after the borrow.

deposit(address,uint256).notice: Deposit `amount` of `asset` into the pool.         Receive lTokens representing the deposit + accrued interest.

initAsset(address).notice: Initialise a new asset in the pool.

repay(address,uint256).notice: Repay up to `amount` of debt on `asset`.         Pass type(uint256).max to repay all outstanding debt.

withdraw(address,uint256).notice: Withdraw up to `amount` of `asset`.         Pass type(uint256).max to withdraw everything.
